data.title: Is Legal Weed In Trouble? Taxes, Oversupply And Hemp Threat Drove A $21-Billion Cut In Sales Estimates
data.description: Whitney Economics cuts U.S. cannabis forecast by $21B, citing taxes, oversupply & lack of federal reform.

data.title: Performance With A Mission: Praxis Enters ETF Arena With Values-Focused Funds
data.description: Praxis Investment Management launches 2 ETFs targeting growth & value with faith-based screens, appealing to investor values & demand for performance.

data.title: Apple Faces Up To 90% Cost Surge On iPhones If US Tariffs Stick: Analyst Looks At Possible Shift To India, Price Increase
data.description: Analyst says there could be an increase in production in India and possible 2-year release cycle for iPhones to mitigate tariffs impact.

data.title: BofA Flags Rising Risks For Gaming And Lodging Firms As Valuations Tumble
data.description: Gaming and lodging stocks are under pressure as recession risks mount, BofA Securities reports. With tariffs weighing on GDP, analysts warn of a potential 3%–8% EBITDA hit. Hotel and timeshare sectors remain the most exposed, while REITs and Gaming OpCos face heightened earnings risk. Stocks are now trading at their lowest valuations since the COVID-19 and GFC downturns.
